mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower real borrowing costs increase present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of 2023-11-15 suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 4.2% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning. Investors reduce equity exposure in anticipation of earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance adjusts.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-11-15 remains below historical median
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.2%, or Rank IC for this signal is negative over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) decelerates below 2% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,200 thousand units, signaling weakening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501,971M and HOUST at 1,287K as of 2023-11-15
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with housing market contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 108.5 index points, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This expansion supports corporate profit margins and cash flow generation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3006 as of 2023-11-15 remains below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 108.5, or Rank IC for this signal is below 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand net new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market slack emerging at the margin. This leading indicator of demand weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100K AND UNRATE stable within ±0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148,662K and UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (PAYEMS deceleration AND UNRATE stability) shows Rank IC below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 1.5%, creating a real rate environment that supports equity valuations. The combination signals monetary policy is restrictive enough to control inflation without crushing growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.535 and FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of 2023-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (real rate proxy) shows Rank IC below 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown and reduced corporate revenue growth. This fundamental demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward across sectors.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or Rank IC for this signal is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat, signaling a supply-side shock with labor hoarding. This combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize margin compression and potential restructuring ahead.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM < -0.5% AND PAYEMS MoM change < 50K
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3006 and PAYEMS at 148,662K as of 2023-11-15
regime_hint: supply shock with labor market rigidity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (supply shock with labor stickiness) shows Rank IC below 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.
